title: Do QuantumScape's Q3 Billings Mark the Start of Monetization?
text: QuantumScape posted a Q3 2025 net loss of $118.7 million, slightly better than Q3 2024's $119.7 million loss, with EPS of -0.20 per share versus the consensus estimate of -0.21. The company moved away from providing updates on cash runway and began providing updates on customer billings, which represent the total value of all invoices issued by QuantumScape to customers and partners in the period. Q3 2025 customer billings of $12.8 million signal a key step toward monetizing its solid-state battery technology. The company achieved all four 2025 goals, including Cobra process baseline, QSE-5 cell shipments, Eagle Line equipment installation, and commercial engagement expansion. The company added two major global automotive OEMs via new joint development and tech evaluation agreements. The successful launch event with Ducati, Audi, and PowerCo at IAA Mobility was a major milestone in the long collaboration with the Volkswagen Group. The company ended the year with $970.8 million in total liquidity and guided to a $250 million to $275 million adjusted EBITDA loss for 2026.
